title: The Lobster
logline: In a dystopian society where single people must find a romantic partner within 45 days or be transformed into an animal, a newly single man escapes to join a group of loners in the woods, only to fall in love and face an impossible choice.
antagonistOrConflict: A society that enforces coupling through the Hotel system and the Loner Leader who forbids romantic relationships
tone: Deadpan, absurdist, clinical, emotionally detached yet deeply tragic
themes: The commodification of love and relationships
The impossibility of authentic connection in oppressive systems

actBreaks.description: David escapes the Hotel after killing his dog and Heartless Woman, fleeing into the woods to join the loners.

validation.ruling: Scene #62 ends Act 1 as David escapes the hotel corridor after shooting Heartless Woman and the Maid, irreversibly abandoning the hotel system and forcing himself into the woods—a fundamental shift from attempting to find a partner within society's rules to becoming a fugitive outside them.

actBreaks.description: Shortsighted Woman is blinded by Loner Leader, destroying the defining characteristic that connected her to David and forcing him to choose between love and survival.

validation.ruling: Scene #106 ends Act 2 with the catastrophic revelation that Shortsighted Woman has been deliberately blinded by Loner Leader, destroying David's escape plan and forcing the final act's impossible choice between abandoning her or blinding himself to match her condition.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a three-world structure disguised as a three-act narrative. Act One (The Hotel) establishes the absurdist rules of forced coupling—David must find a partner or become a lobster. The world is rigidly controlled, emotionally sterile, and darkly comic. Act Two (The Woods) inverts the first act's tyranny: the loners forbid romance entirely under Loner Leader's authoritarian rule. David finds genuine connection with Shortsighted Woman, but their love is illegal in this new system. Act Three (The Town/Return) collapses both worlds—David and Shortsighted Woman escape to civilian life, but when she's blinded, their defining compatibility vanishes. The script's genius lies in its symmetry: both the Hotel and the Loners are totalitarian systems that deny authentic human connection, just from opposite directions. The final scene leaves David frozen in a restaurant bathroom, unable to blind himself, unable to return to her—trapped between two impossible choices.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The first act moves with methodical precision through 62 scenes establishing the Hotel's bizarre rituals—hunts, lectures, forced compatibility tests. The pacing is deliberately slow and observational, building absurdist dread. Act Two accelerates once David enters the woods (scene 63), compressing his romance with Shortsighted Woman into intimate, stolen moments between Loner Leader's surveillance. The blinding (scene 106) functions as a devastating pivot that drains all momentum—the final 17 scenes slow to an agonizing crawl as David realizes he cannot replicate her sacrifice. The restaurant sequence (scenes 121-123) stretches a single moment across three scenes, ending on David's paralysis rather than resolution.

incitingIncident.description: David sits crying on a sofa while his wife stands by a bookcase and tells him she's leaving him for another man who also wears glasses.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. TOWN HOUSE SITTING ROOM - MORNING

validation.ruling: Scene #2 shatters David's ordinary world when his wife confesses her affair with another shortsighted man and forces him to leave, establishing the central conflict that drives him to the hotel system where singles must find partners or be transformed into animals.

midpoint.description: Loner Swimmer brings David to the Loner Leader who questions him about his profession and medical needs, then explains the strict rules against flirting, sexual contact, and masturbation in the loner community.

validation.ruling: Scene #65 marks David's shift from reactive hotel guest to proactive loner when he joins the autonomous forest community, crossing a point of no return by rejecting the hotel's coupling system entirely and embracing a new ideology with strict anti-romantic rules.

climax.description: Shortsighted Woman sits alone at the restaurant table waiting as the waiter fills her glass, couples pass by the window, and time passes.

validation.ruling: Scene #122 shows David preparing to blind himself but is setup, not resolution. Scene #123 is the true climax where Shortsighted Woman waits alone at the restaurant table as time passes—the ultimate confrontation with whether David will complete his self-blinding to match her disability and commit to their relationship, resolving the central question of connection versus autonomy.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: A woman drives to a deserted country road, checks for witnesses, runs to three donkeys, shoots one repeatedly, and drives away.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN HOUSE SITTING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David sits crying on a sofa while his wife stands by a bookcase and tells him she's leaving him for another man who also wears glasses.
scenes.turningPointNote: David's marriage ends, triggering his entry into the Hotel system — the inciting incident of the story.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN HOUSE BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David slowly gets dressed in his bedroom, staring at his slippers and deciding not to take them, while a voiceover describes the other man as very handsome.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN HOUSE SITTING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David descends the stairs and sees an unattractive man eating a tuna sandwich in his sitting room while his wife brings the man a Coca-Cola.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN HOUSE SITTING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David sits in the armchair avoiding eye contact with the other man while his wife watches the window and everyone waits in silence until two waiters arrive.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN ROAD - MORNING
scenes.summary: The waiters lead David to a coach, and his dog follows them into the vehicle.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL COACH - MORNING
scenes.summary: David boards a coach with four other people and his dog, and Biscuit Woman approaches to stroke the dog and offer him biscuits.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL COACH - MORNING
scenes.summary: David looks out the coach window as rocks suddenly strike it and people in ponchos run alongside shouting, but the driver speeds away unfazed.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL CARPARK - MORNING
scenes.summary: The coach passes through hotel gates opened by armed waiter-guards and stops at a large fenced hotel complex where people watch from balconies.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: The new arrivals stand in the empty hotel lobby decorated with paintings of couples and animals while the receptionist welcomes them and asks if anyone is homosexual.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL LOBBY - MORNING
scenes.summary: David checks in at reception while his anxious dog barks continuously, and he reveals the dog is his brother who failed at the hotel and was transformed.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that David's brother was transformed into the dog establishes the stakes of failure at the Hotel — a fate worse than death.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL RECEPTION ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: David undresses and surrenders his clothes to staff, receives hotel shoes in whole sizes only, and is given a choice of four CDs for his room.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - MORNING
scenes.summary: David enters his single room in his underwear with the dog and sees a tranquilliser gun hanging above the bed with twenty darts on the bedside table.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - MORNING
scenes.summary: David watches from his window as hotel residents dump unconscious bodies outside, then receives a phone call from Biscuit Woman describing herself and asking about him.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Hotel Manager and her husband visit David's room to explain the rules: he has 45 days to find a partner or be transformed into an animal of his choice.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Hotel Manager explicitly states the central conflict and ticking clock — David must couple within 45 days or face transformation.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David struggles to squeeze toothpaste onto his toothbrush and screw the cap back on with one hand tied behind his back, then brushes his teeth.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David struggles to remove his trousers with the belt still on and one hand restrained, eventually managing to free one leg after lying down.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - MORNING
scenes.summary: David wakes to a love song, having managed to remove his trousers overnight, fixes his hair lifting the trousers still attached to his wrist, and gives the dog water.

scenes.heading: INT. BREAKFAST ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David enters the breakfast room wearing his trousers normally and walks quickly past staring residents to find an empty table, noticing all new arrivals have arms tied behind their backs.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - MORNING
scenes.summary: Limping Man, David, and Lisping Man sit on the beach near guarded yachts while Lisping Man gossips about other hotel residents' relationships and age differences.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL - MIDDAY
scenes.summary: Limping Man speaks into a microphone on stage, telling the audience how his mother was transformed into a wolf after his father left her for a better math player.

scenes.heading: EXT. ZOO - DAY
scenes.summary: Limping Man walks normally through a zoo carrying raw meat, throws it to wolves in an enclosure, unable to identify which wolf is his mother.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL - MIDDAY
scenes.summary: The audience applauds Limping Man's story, then another guest describes his nosebleeds as his defining characteristic and reads reference letters praising his sensitivity.

scenes.heading: INT. BALLROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: At a dance, Biscuit Woman is rejected by Heartless Woman and joins Nosebleed Woman's table as third in line, while Nosebleed Woman explains Heartless Woman feels nothing at all.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - EVENING
scenes.summary: David rushes into his room, takes the tranquilliser gun off the wall, loads it, and leaves while the dog watches without reacting.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL COACH - EVENING
scenes.summary: David boards the coach and sits next to nervous Limping Man, advising him to use surprise tactics instead of speed during the hunt.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Hotel residents armed with tranquilliser guns and torches exit the coach and run into the woods to hunt loners while waiters remain by the vehicle.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Limping Man struggles to run and fails to climb rocks during the hunt.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Heartless Woman spots a young loner woman eating nuts, chases her down, beats her unconscious, then shoots her with the tranquilliser gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Two hotel guests stand over an unconscious loner and begin dragging the body in different directions before stopping to hit each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: David spots a loner man, shoots and misses repeatedly, chases him until running out of breath and darts, then sits on a rock checking his watch.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL ENTRANCE - EVENING
scenes.summary: The maid counts captured loners and announces extra days earned, giving Heartless Woman four days and telling David he has 43 days remaining with no captures.

scenes.heading: EXT. BREAKFAST ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Hotel Manager confronts Lisping Man about masturbating in the shower, and after he admits it, she orders a waiter to burn his hand on a toaster.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Hotel Manager stages a reenactment showing a man choking alone versus being saved by a woman, then demonstrates a woman being raped alone versus with a man present.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - MIDDAY
scenes.summary: David's elderly parents visit his room, his mother insisting the dog is his father and that it wags its tail happily, while they discuss the room and David's situation.

scenes.heading: INT. BREAKFAST ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: David tries to cheer up Limping Man by mentioning a new woman with a limp, but Limping Man reveals it's only a sprained ankle that will heal.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL COACH - MORNING
scenes.summary: Biscuit Woman sits next to David on the coach, asks about Bob, offers him biscuits for the dog, and suggests they could walk together sometime.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MIDDAY
scenes.summary: Lisping Man discovers a young loner woman with a lisp hiding behind a tree and, realizing they share this trait, decides to capture her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lisping Man finds a potential match in a loner woman, creating a forbidden romance subplot that challenges the Hotel's rules.

scenes.heading: INT. COACH - MIDDAY
scenes.summary: David and Lisping Man sit together on the coach while Lisping Man excitedly describes how the captured woman lisps and has matching blonde hair, hoping for an exception.

scenes.heading: INT. INDOOR SHOOTING RANGE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Lisping Man tells David and Limping Man that the Hotel Manager suggested keeping the loner woman as a pet in his room and transforming her into a dog.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Maid makes David's bed, replenishes his tranquilliser darts, then orders him to remove his trousers so she can sexually stimulate him without allowing release.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL POOL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Limping Man approaches Nosebleed Woman in the pool and they discover they both suffer from sudden nosebleeds, bonding over this shared characteristic.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - EVENING
scenes.summary: Limping Man visits David's room to say goodbye, bangs his nose against the wall to make it bleed, and asks whether transformation or pretending is worse.
scenes.turningPointNote: Limping Man's desperate act of self-harm to fake a matching characteristic reveals the psychological breaking point and moral compromise required to survive the Hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL RECEPTION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Hotel Manager presents Limping Man and Nosebleed Woman as a new couple matched by their nosebleeds, and they receive a room key while Nosebleed Woman's Best Friend cries.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL ENTRANCE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: David, Nosebleed Woman, her Best Friend, and Limping Man look at children in an enclosure, and one child approaches to advertise himself for adoption.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL CORRIDOR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: David compliments Nosebleed Woman's Best Friend's hair and she criticizes his, then he mentions baldness treatments and she says she finds bald men sexy.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL BAR - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Hotel Manager tells Nosebleed Woman's Best Friend it's her last day and to choose a final activity, then Nosebleed Woman reads an emotional letter before her friend is transformed.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - DAY
scenes.summary: David watches from his window as the Maid releases a pony into the grounds while Nosebleed Woman and Limping Man wave goodbye, then his phone rings unanswered.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASKETBALL COURT - DAY
scenes.summary: David watches a couple playing basketball while a voiceover describes a woman's experience at the hotel and how she escaped by letting a waiter have sex with her.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL GROUNDS - DAY
scenes.summary: David sees a crowd around Biscuit Woman lying bloody and screaming on the ground after jumping from a balcony, and he tells Heartless Woman he hopes she dies.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: David plays Biscuit Woman's final answering machine message repeatedly while his dog listens beside him, showing visible regret over her suicide.

scenes.heading: INT. BALLROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: David approaches Heartless Woman at a table in the ballroom; she chokes on a cherry and her body convulses while David does nothing to help her.

scenes.heading: INT. DOUBLE ROOM 202 - EVENING
scenes.summary: David and Heartless Woman move into a double room together with the dog, discussing which side of the bed they prefer and settling in as a matched couple.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL GARDENS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David, Heartless Woman, and the dog walk hand-in-hand through the hotel gardens and encounter Limping Man with his family; David coldly refuses to kiss the little girl.

scenes.heading: INT. DOUBLE ROOM 202 - DAY
scenes.summary: Heartless Woman wakes David to tell him she kicked his brother (the dog) to death, showing him her blood-covered shoe while David pretends indifference despite being devastated.
scenes.turningPointNote: David's brother is killed by Heartless Woman, shattering his fake relationship and setting up his violent retaliation and escape from the hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL CORRIDORS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Heartless Woman drags David by the hair through hotel corridors until he kicks her in the stomach and escapes into the lift while she runs up the stairs.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL FIRST-FLOOR CORRIDOR - EVENING
scenes.summary: Heartless Woman searches doors on the first floor and discovers a woman performing sexual acts with three waiters in a room.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL STAIRS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Heartless Woman exits the room and calmly walks up to the second floor, appearing determined but not angry or nervous.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL SECOND-FLOOR CORRIDOR - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Maid misdirects Heartless Woman, then David emerges and shoots Heartless Woman with a tranquilizer gun while the Maid helps him lift her unconscious body.

scenes.heading: INT. ANIMAL TRANSFORMATION ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: David shoots the Maid with the tranquilizer gun so she won't be suspected of helping him, then shuts himself in the animal transformation room with Heartless Woman.
scenes.turningPointNote: David transforms Heartless Woman into an animal and prepares to escape the hotel permanently, crossing a moral line and ending his time in the hotel system.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 101 - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Hotel Manager discovers the dead dog in David's room and orders waiters to sound the alarm while Bon Jovi plays throughout the building.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL CORRIDOR - EVENING
scenes.summary: David cautiously disappears down the hotel corridor as the alarm sounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David walks quickly through the woods, stops to wash his face and clean his glasses at a river, then lies down exhausted but unable to relax.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David washes his hair in the river when Loner Swimmer appears; the swimmer initially thinks David is a hunter but David explains he escaped from the hotel after his time ran out.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Loner Swimmer brings David to the Loner Leader who questions him about his profession and medical needs, then explains the strict rules against flirting, sexual contact, and masturbation in the loner community.
scenes.turningPointNote: David enters the loner community and learns their rigid anti-romantic rules, establishing the new world he must navigate and the central conflict of Act 2.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Loner Swimmer gives David supplies including a cape, CD player, solitaire game, book, and knife; David notices Bandaged Loner has fresh blood on his mouth bandages and learns he was punished with the Red Kiss for kissing another loner.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David applies Tiger Balm cream to his upper back but cannot reach most of the area properly.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Loner Leader walks a pig through the woods on a lead, stopping wherever the animal pauses to dig in the ground with her hands before continuing.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: The loners exercise in rows while Loner Leader corrects their technique; Shortsighted Woman exercises while looking at David three rows behind her.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David hides in the woods during a hunt and is discovered by Lisping Man, who accuses him of turning Heartless Woman into a dog and kicking her to death; David claims she killed his brother and calls Lisping Man his best friend to save himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. A CLEARING IN THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Maid tells the driver waiter she's going truffle hunting and needs a pig, then leaves with a bag over her back.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Maid meets Loner Leader in the woods and gives her a gun, key, and folder with double room numbers, suggesting Saturday for an attack when staff are tired and off work.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Loners rest after a hunt while David sits with Loner Leader, who tells him they're going to town tomorrow and he'll pretend to be Shortsighted Woman's partner.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS, TREE HOLLOW - EVENING
scenes.summary: Loner Leader gives David civilian clothes from a tree hollow, handing him a suit that fits.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE TOWN - MORNING
scenes.summary: David and Shortsighted Woman walk arm-in-arm through town pretending to be a couple alongside Loner Leader and Loner Swimmer, browsing shop windows and appearing happy.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOP - MORNING
scenes.summary: The group buys knives, trainers, bandages, a watch, and orange juice cartons in a shop.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHOP - MORNING
scenes.summary: David watches police officers arrest a blonde woman whose certificate expired two months ago, checking her nails and shoes for dirt before taking her away.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE TOWN - MORNING
scenes.summary: David and Shortsighted Woman walk hand-in-hand on the street, adjusting their grip and discussing their pace and shopping plans in careful, measured conversation.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: The four loners arrive at a house where Loner Leader's elderly father opens the door and hugs her.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Loner Leader talks to a dog in the garden while the others drink orange squash; she returns to the table and tells her father they're leaving today because she has a business trip tomorrow.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Walking back through town, Loner Leader praises David's performance on his first day, particularly his comments about Mediterranean holidays; Shortsighted Woman tightens her grip on David's hand.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David sets up rabbit traps in the woods and successfully catches three rabbits, which he prepares according to a recipe described in voiceover.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David wakes to his alarm, goes to wash his face, and discovers a note written on his hand directing him to meet at the river bank beside a tree at 11 a.m., which he rubs off while looking around.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David and Shortsighted Woman sit alone together for the first time beside a seal, awkwardly discussing when they became shortsighted and their astigmatism; she offers to rub balm on his back.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Loner Leader distributes hotel guest clothes and pistols to the loners, then reads room numbers and couple characteristics from the Maid's folder for their planned raid.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: The loners walk through the woods with flashlights in silence, led by Loner Leader with Shortsighted Woman following and David looking worried at the back.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL - EVENING
scenes.summary: The loners enter the hotel gardens through a small gate, hide behind bushes, and Loner Leader assigns them to different rooms; David volunteers to go to the pier instead of room 282.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 206 - EVENING
scenes.summary: Loner Leader, Loner Swimmer, and Shortsighted Woman stand over a naked couple in bed with guns; Loner Leader questions them about their singing while Shortsighted Woman steals CDs and a notebook.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL GROUNDS - EVENING
scenes.summary: David walks quickly with a tranquilizer gun and stops in front of hotel signage pointing toward the beach, pier, and mini golf.

scenes.heading: EXT. PIER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Two waiters with automatic rifles guard yachts at the pier, discussing customer service complaints while appearing relaxed and not very alert.

scenes.heading: INT. YACHT - EVENING
scenes.summary: David climbs aboard a yacht and looks through the window at Limping Man, Nosebleed Woman, and their daughter Elizabeth in matching nautical outfits, discussing the weight of basketballs and volleyballs.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: The loners dance individually to electronic music while David searches for Shortsighted Woman; Loner Leader approaches and reminds him he needs to dig his own grave.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: David sees Rabbit Loner bringing rabbits to Shortsighted Woman and making her laugh; David approaches her upset and asks who brought the rabbits, denying he's jealous when she asks.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: David confronts Rabbit Loner while he's shaving, accusing him of being shortsighted and lying about it by testing him with his jacket zipper.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Shortsighted Woman stand beside a pony and synchronize their CD players, counting down together before pressing play and dancing to the same song playing slightly out of sync.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Various scenes of loner life play while voiceover describes the secret code David and Shortsighted Woman developed to communicate their love without others knowing.

scenes.heading: INT. LONER LEADER’S PARENTS’ HOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: At Loner Leader's parents' house, the elderly couple tells stories about meeting in school and bonding over music while David and Shortsighted Woman sit in a corner.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN STREET - MORNING
scenes.summary: Walking back through town, David tries to gauge if Loner Leader suspects them by commenting on their performance and the parents' singing, while he and Shortsighted Woman smile but appear worried.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: The loners eat fruit together while David covers his left eye and Shortsighted Woman does a handstand, using their secret code as a pink flamingo stands in the distance.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: A Loner Woman reads from a notebook to Loner Leader, revealing a written confession that two loners love each other and plan to escape to town during tomorrow's hunt.
scenes.turningPointNote: David and Shortsighted Woman's secret relationship is discovered through their written confession, triggering the climactic crisis that will determine their fate.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Loner Leader approaches Shortsighted Woman reading by torchlight and asks her for a sheet of white paper to make a shopping list.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTOR’S SURGERY WAITING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Loner Leader, Loner Narrator, and Shortsighted Woman wait in a doctor's surgery; Shortsighted Woman expresses fear about being suspected but Loner Leader dismisses her concerns.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTOR’S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Doctor explains to Loner Leader and Shortsighted Woman that the eye surgery is routine with a 90% success rate and involves laser, not scalpels.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATING THEATRE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Shortsighted Woman lies frightened on the operating table as the Doctor anaesthetizes her and lays out scalpels on a metal tray.

scenes.heading: INT. DOCTOR’S SURGERY WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Doctor brings out Shortsighted Woman with bandaged eyes and whispers to Loner Leader that she is blind as wished.
scenes.turningPointNote: Loner Leader's deliberate blinding of Shortsighted Woman is revealed, fundamentally altering the power dynamic and Shortsighted Woman's fate.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Shortsighted Woman, still bandaged and unable to see, kneels on the ground threatening with a knife while Loner Leader denies intentionally blinding her and claims the doctor reported unforeseen complications.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: David meets Shortsighted Woman who now wears sunglasses; she tries to hide her blindness but drops the act and tells David she is blind and they cannot escape together.
scenes.turningPointNote: David discovers Shortsighted Woman's blindness, destroying their escape plan and fundamentally changing their relationship.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Loner Leader invites David to accompany her truffle hunting with a pig, and despite being sleepy, David gets up and goes with her.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David walks through the woods with Loner Leader who tells him about Shortsighted Woman's blindness and claims her deputy leader was killed by a jaguar on the way back from town.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David brings carrots and fish to Shortsighted Woman and asks her questions about blood type, berries, piano, and German, receiving negative answers to all.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS, MIDDAY
scenes.summary: David again brings carrots and fish to Shortsighted Woman and asks the same questions about blood type, berries, piano, and German, receiving the same negative answers.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David plays a guessing game with Shortsighted Woman using objects; she correctly identifies a plastic cup but mistakes a tennis ball for a kiwi, then refuses when David suggests making a fist behind his back.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: David sings a duet alone, only performing the male part, and encounters a rabbit but does not kill it.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - DAY
scenes.summary: Loner Leader brings David to a quiet spot in the woods and tells him it is the perfect place for his grave, threatening to bury him alive.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David encounters a pony in the forest, cuts its mane with scissors, and hits it across the face.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS, MORNING
scenes.summary: David approaches Shortsighted Woman and other loners setting a rabbit trap and takes her aside to describe an idea using their physical code.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: David attacks and knocks unconscious Loner Leader in the dark, then ties her up with gaffer tape and drags her away.
scenes.turningPointNote: David violently overthrows Loner Leader, eliminating the primary obstacle to his and Shortsighted Woman's escape.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Loner Leader lies tied up in David's freshly dug grave while dogs circle the shallow pit and she tries unsuccessfully to cut herself loose.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE WOODS - MORNING
scenes.summary: David and Shortsighted Woman walk quickly through the woods in civilian clothes, with David leading and Shortsighted Woman holding his hand as they head toward the city.
scenes.turningPointNote: David and Shortsighted Woman successfully escape the woods together, leaving the Loner society behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN ROAD - MIDDAY
scenes.summary: David and Shortsighted Woman emerge onto the main road and hide in bushes as the hotel coach passes, then continue toward town.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: David and Shortsighted Woman sit silently at a restaurant table where David asks her to show him her profile, fingers, and elbows.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN RESTAURANT TOILETS - DAY
scenes.summary: David prepares himself in the restaurant bathroom by laying paper towels over his shoulders, then raises a knife close to his eye but cannot go through with it and tries again.
scenes.turningPointNote: David attempts to blind himself to match Shortsighted Woman's defining characteristic, the ultimate irreversible act to maintain their relationship in a society that demands shared traits.

scenes.heading: INT. TOWN RESTAURANT - DAY
scenes.summary: Shortsighted Woman sits alone at the restaurant table waiting as the waiter fills her glass, couples pass by the window, and time passes.
